title: Knowing God as Father. part 1
scripture_text: ‘’Be careful not to practice your righteousness in front of others to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven. So when you give to the needy, do not announce it with trumpets, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be honoured by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full, but when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing, so that your giving may be in secret. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you. And when you pray, do not be like the hypocrites, for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full. But when you pray, go into your room, close the door and pray to your Father, who is unseen. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you. And when you pray, do not keep on babbling like pagans, for they think they will be heard because of their many words. Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him. This, then, is how you should pray: “‘Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name your kingdom come, your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven. Give us today our daily bread. And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors., And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one. For if you forgive other people when they sin against you, your heavenly Father will also forgive you. But if you do not forgive others their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins. “When you fast, do not look sombre as the hypocrites do, for they disfigure their faces to show others they are fasting. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full. But when you fast, put oil on your head and wash your face, so that it will not be obvious to others that you are fasting, but only to your Father, who is unseen; and your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you. “Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moths and vermin destroy and where thieves break in and steal, But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven where moths and vermin do not destroy, and where thieves do not break in and steal. For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also. “The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eyes are healthy, your whole body will be full of light. But if your eyes are unhealthy, our whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light within you is darkness, how great is that darkness! “No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes? Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them Are you not much more valuable than they? Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life? And why do you worry about clothes? See how the flowers of the field grow. They do not labour or spin. Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendour was dressed like one of these. If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the fire, will he not much more clothe you—you of little faith? So do not worry, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear. For the pagans run after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them. But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well. Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own
devotional:
Matthew 6 begins with two very important words, which are “Be careful,’ It would be tempting to just read over these two words and miss what they are saying. “Be Careful is one word in the Greek, PROSECHO. It means to “be continually guarding against.” It has the idea of continually watching yourself. It was used to describe the skill of a sea captain who was able to bring his vessel into harbour without beaching it on the hidden shallow rocks.

Remember the first disciples were fishermen, who would have been able to relate to what Jesus is saying here. So when we read “Be Careful,” Jesus is wanting to get our attention, he is saying that if we do not hear what he is going to say, we are in danger of beaching our faith and shipwrecking our testimonies.

One of the ways we shipwreck our faith is sometimes we forget who we really are, we forget that we are children of the living God. Sometimes the storms of life cause us to hit the rocks and we feel that we have been ‘beached’ or stuck and we can’t move.

It is so easy to react to the storms of life by feeling that I must try harder, I must pray more, and I must read the bible more so I can get through this crisis. Spiritual disciplines are always a good thing. However in times of crisis we need more than spiritual disciplines and self effort, we need a fresh revelation of God.

Every commentary I have read on Matthew 6 believes that Jesus is teaching his disciples how to have healthy wholesome Christian disciplines. So he talks about how they should give to the poor, how they should pray and how they should fast. Jesus is teaching how to cultivate a proper heart attitude so they can participate in the natural spiritual requirements of a follower of Jesus. The more I have looked at the chapter, the more I am inclined to think that the giving, praying and fasting, which are extremely important, are secondary issues as far as Matthew 6 is concerned. There is a much more important issue that is not often picked up.

If you read the whole Sermon on the Mount especially chapter 6 you will find one word that is repeated 17 times, 11 of which are in chapter 6. That word is Father. The word father is found more in Matthew 6 than any other chapter in the bible therefore it has a lot to teach us about God as our father, and what it means to be his child.

It’s really important to God that we have a revelation of what it means to have Him as our father. How we live and what we live for is determined by our understanding of the fatherhood of God and the security it produces in our lives.
prayer: Father may my spiritual disciplines grow out of me knowing you as my Father.
html:
Knowing God as Father. part 1

Study Text: Matthew 6
‘’Be careful not to practice your righteousness in front of others to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven. So when you give to the needy, do not announce it with trumpets, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be honoured by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full, but when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing, so that your giving may be in secret. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you. And when you pray, do not be like the hypocrites, for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full. But when you pray, go into your room, close the door and pray to your Father, who is unseen. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you. And when you pray, do not keep on babbling like pagans, for they think they will be heard because of their many words. Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him. This, then, is how you should pray: “‘Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name your kingdom come, your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven. Give us today our daily bread. And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors., And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one. But if you do not forgive others their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins. “When you fast, do not look sombre as the hypocrites do, for they disfigure their faces to show others they are fasting. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full. For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also. “The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eyes are healthy, your whole body will be full of light. But if your eyes are unhealthy, our whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light within you is darkness, how great is that darkness! “No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes? Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them Are you not much more valuable than they? Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life? And why do you worry about clothes? See how the flowers of the field grow. They do not labour or spin. Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendour was dressed like one of these. If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the fire, will he not much more clothe you—you of little faith? So do not worry, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear. For the pagans run after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them. But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well. Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own

Matthew 6 begins with two very important words, which are “Be careful,’ It would be tempting to just read over these two words and miss what they are saying. “Be Careful is one word in the Greek, PROSECHO. It means to “be continually guarding against.” It has the idea of continually watching yourself. It was used to describe the skill of a sea captain who was able to bring his vessel into harbour without beaching it on the hidden shallow rocks.

So when we read “Be Careful,” Jesus is wanting to get our attention, he is saying that if we do not hear what he is going to say, we are in danger of beaching our faith and shipwrecking our testimonies.

One of the ways we shipwreck our faith is sometimes we forget who we really are, we forget that we are children of the living God. Sometimes the storms of life cause us to hit the rocks and we feel that we have been ‘beached’ or stuck and we can’t move.

It is so easy to react to the storms of life by feeling that I must try harder, I must pray more, and I must read the bible more so I can get through this crisis. Spiritual disciplines are always a good thing. However in times of crisis we need more than spiritual disciplines and self effort, we need a fresh revelation of God.

Every commentary I have read on Matthew 6 believes that Jesus is teaching his disciples how to have healthy wholesome Christian disciplines. So he talks about how they should give to the poor, how they should pray and how they should fast. Jesus is teaching how to cultivate a proper heart attitude so they can participate in the natural spiritual requirements of a follower of Jesus. The more I have looked at the chapter, the more I am inclined to think that the giving, praying and fasting, which are extremely important, are secondary issues as far as Matthew 6 is concerned. There is a much more important issue that is not often picked up.

If you read the whole Sermon on the Mount especially chapter 6 you will find one word that is repeated 17 times, 11 of which are in chapter 6. That word is Father. The word father is found more in Matthew 6 than any other chapter in the bible therefore it has a lot to teach us about God as our father, and what it means to be his child.

It’s really important to God that we have a revelation of what it means to have Him as our father. How we live and what we live for is determined by our understanding of the fatherhood of God and the security it produces in our lives.

Prayer: Father may my spiritual disciplines grow out of me knowing you as my Father.
